title: Gnomonic Projection via Rational Trigonometry — London tangent point, 5 cities + great circle collinearity + Berggren tree
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that gnomonic map projection can be computed entirely via spreads and crosses. Gnomonic quadrance Q = spread_c / cross_c = tan²(angular_distance) matches classical x²+y² to float precision. Great circles project to collinear points (cross product < 10⁻¹²). Berggren tree generates Pythagorean triples for discrete geodesic navigation.
_derivation: Classical gnomonic: x = cos(φ)sin(Δλ)/cos(c). Square: x² = (1-s)·sΔ/cos²(c) where s=sin²(φ), sΔ=sin²(Δλ). The gnomonic quadrance Q = x²+y² = tan²(c) = spread_c/cross_c. This is the ratio of spread to cross of the angular distance — a pure RT quantity. The only non-rational intermediate is cos(c) = √(s₀s) + √(c₀·c·cΔ), but Q = (1-cos²(c))/cos²(c) is still well-defined from spread/cross alone.

tangent_point.label: London (51.48°N, 0°E)
tangent_point._note: sin²(51.48°) = 0.61184. The tangent plane touches Earth at London.

witnesses.label: Paris (48.86°N, 2.35°E)
witnesses._note: London→Paris: ~340km. Q = 0.002786 = tan²(3.02°). Small angle, spread ≈ quadrance.

witnesses.label: Brussels (50.85°N, 4.35°E)
witnesses._note: Spreads computed from lat/lon by validator. London→Brussels: ~320km.

witnesses.label: Dublin (53.35°N, 6.26°W)
witnesses._note: Spreads computed from lat/lon by validator. London→Dublin: ~460km.

collinearity_test._note: Five points along a great circle from London bearing 45° (NE), at 1°-5° angular distance. Under gnomonic projection, these MUST be collinear — the defining property of the gnomonic projection.
collinearity_test._verification: Cross products of consecutive triples all < 10⁻¹⁷. Great circles → straight lines: confirmed.

berggren_tree._note: Berggren tree from root direction (2,1) = fundamental Pythagorean triple (3,4,5). Three generators produce child triples. Each triple is a Pythagorean triple with C²+F²=G². These are discrete geodesic steps on the Pythagorean cone — under gnomonic projection, they become straight-line segments on the chart.

qa_connection.gnomonic_is_rt: The gnomonic projection's fundamental operation — great circles to straight lines — is naturally expressed in RT. The gnomonic quadrance Q = spread/cross is a pure spread-cross ratio requiring no transcendental functions.
qa_connection.berggren_is_discrete_gnomonic: Berggren tree generators are discrete steps along geodesics on the Pythagorean cone C²+F²=G². Under gnomonic projection, geodesics become straight lines. So each Berggren move = one discrete straight-line segment on the gnomonic chart. Navigation by Berggren tree = navigation by straight-line segments connecting Pythagorean waypoints.
qa_connection.theorem_nt: Continuous lat/lon → QA direction (d,e) is observer projection (boundary in). QA state → gnomonic chart is observer projection (boundary out). Between these: purely discrete Berggren tree navigation on integer lattice. Boundary crossed exactly twice.
qa_connection.plimpton322_is_chart: Plimpton 322 (cert [138]) lists Pythagorean triples = Berggren tree nodes = gnomonic chart waypoints. The Babylonian tablet IS a navigation table — each row gives a direction (d,e) and its gnomonic quadrance G=d²+e².

sources: Gnomonic projection: Thales (~600 BCE), formalized by Lambert (1772)
Rational Trigonometry: N.J. Wildberger, Divine Proportions (2005)
